title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: So today, I'm going to show you my winter routine.
I'm going to get the snow blower.
I'm going to use a measuring cup to measure out 24 ounces of chicken broth.
So we're going to head home and get nice and warm so we don't catch a cold.
I'm going to make a snow angel and build a snowman.

title: will / shall
examples: I usually dress in layers, so I'll have two pairs of pants on three shirts.
Let it cook all day and at night, you'll have a meal ready to eat.
We have all of the ingredients here and we will just throw it in the crock pot and let it cook all day.
And now I am covering it and we will cook it on high for three to four hours.
That will be perfect because when we come back in after sledding, it'll be all ready to eat.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: Usually, when we get snow like this, I check the news right away to see how the road conditions are.
Before I go outside, I have to bundle up in winter.
That will be perfect because when we come back in after sledding, it'll be all ready to eat.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: So you can make it in the morning.
You can tell me it's okay.
A can of corn.
I don't know if you can see what's behind me here, but we're actually going sledding.
You can see the wings.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: You can't have hot chocolate without some marshmallows.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: So this might be a little bit of a struggle.
Now I'm freezing cold and I feel like I might have frostbite.

title: have to / must
examples: But I have to stick to some kind of a routine.
Before I go outside, I have to bundle up in winter.
I think I have to throw my clothes in the dryer.
Even Willie had to put his sweater on.
But still, you have to be very careful, drive slow, and look out for black ice.

title: should (advice)
examples: I should be wearing snow pants for this but I'm going to make a snow angel.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: And when I was younger, I would always check to see if there were any school closings.
On a day like today, most people would not leave the house because the road conditions are not the greatest.
I wouldn't say like one better than the other because they are so different.
It's not as spicy as I thought it would be, but it's got some good flavor to it.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: The very first thing I do every single morning in winter is put on my slippers because my feet are always freezing cold in winter.
Luckily, my mom is very experienced when it comes to winter driving because she was born and raised in Michigan.

title: Passive (been done / being done)
examples: That will be perfect because when we come back in after sledding, it'll be all ready to eat.

title: have something done
examples: We have a curing at my mom's house, so I just take one pot of coffee, put it in the machine.
I guess my mom is telling me to get my butt out there in shovel.
We've got a classic sled here, which is similar to a toboggan and my favorite, a saucer.
I even got a real carrot for the nose.
We've got a raspberry flavored one and a salted caramel flavored one.
